translation: day (24 hours, usually from midnight to midnight)
year, the time it takes a planetary body to complete one revolution around a star
leg (of a person, animal, humanoid robot, puppet, etc.)
tea (the dried leaves or buds of the tea plant, Camellia sinensis)
ear (of a human, animal, puppet, humanoid robot, etc.)
night, nighttime (period of time from sundown to sunup)
fish (cold-blooded vertebrate animal that lives in water, moving with the help of fins and breathing with gills)
book (a collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge)
love (intense feeling of affection and care toward another person)
trade (commercial exchange of goods and services)
room (separate part of a building, enclosed by walls, a floor and a ceiling)
grammar school (UK) (secondary school that stresses academic over practical or vocational education)
girlfriend (female partner in a romantic relationship)
exercise book (a booklet for students, containing blank, lined or checkered pages for writing answers, drawing graphs etc.)
